evals.prompt:
I ran astrodb-parse-data-table on my catalog and got this output. Can you tell me which AstroDB table and field each column belongs to?

| Column | Description | Units | Type |
|--------|-------------|-------|------|
| source_id | Primary source identifier | — | str |
| alt_name | Alternative catalog name | — | str |
| ra | Right ascension (J2000) | deg | float64 |
| dec | Declination (J2000) | deg | float64 |
| plx | Parallax | mas | float64 |
| e_plx | Parallax uncertainty | mas | float64 |
| pmRA | Proper motion in RA*cos(dec) | mas/yr | float64 |
| e_pmRA | Uncertainty in pmRA | mas/yr | float64 |
| pmDE | Proper motion in declination | mas/yr | float64 |
| e_pmDE | Uncertainty in pmDE | mas/yr | float64 |
| RV | Heliocentric radial velocity | km/s | float64 |
| e_RV | RV uncertainty | km/s | float64 |
| SpT | Spectral type | — | str |
| assoc | Moving group or association membership | — | str |
| ref | Reference bibcode | — | str |
evals.expected_output: A markdown table mapping each column to the correct AstroDB table and field with confidence levels and ingestion notes.

assertions.text: All 15 input columns appear in the output table and each has a non-empty DB Table and DB Field assignment (no blanks or N/A for well-known columns)
Column 'plx' is mapped to Parallaxes table and parallax_mas field
Column 'e_plx' is mapped to Parallaxes table and parallax_error field
Column 'pmRA' is mapped to ProperMotions table and pm_ra field
Column 'pmDE' is mapped to ProperMotions table and pm_dec field
Column 'RV' is mapped to RadialVelocities table and rv_kms field
Column 'SpT' is mapped to SourceTypes table and source_type field
Column 'assoc' is mapped to Associations table
Column 'source_id' is mapped to Sources table and source field
Column 'alt_name' is mapped to Names table and other_name field
The ingestion notes section mentions that each data table row requires a source foreign key (source identifier is required in each data table)

evals.prompt:
Here's the column table from astrodb-parse-data-table for a multi-band photometry catalog. Please map these to the right AstroDB fields:

| Column | Description | Units | Type |
|--------|-------------|-------|------|
| name | Object designation (2MASS or WISE) | — | str |
| Jmag | 2MASS J-band magnitude | mag | float64 |
| eJmag | J-band magnitude uncertainty | mag | float64 |
| Hmag | 2MASS H-band magnitude | mag | float64 |
| eHmag | H-band uncertainty | mag | float64 |
| Kmag | 2MASS Ks-band magnitude | mag | float64 |
| eKmag | Ks-band uncertainty | mag | float64 |
| W1mag | WISE W1 magnitude | mag | float64 |
| eW1mag | W1 uncertainty | mag | float64 |
| W2mag | WISE W2 magnitude | mag | float64 |
| eW2mag | W2 uncertainty | mag | float64 |
| Teff | Effective temperature from SED fit | K | float64 |
| e_Teff | Teff uncertainty | K | float64 |
| logL | Log luminosity from SED fit | Lsun | float64 |
| e_logL | logL uncertainty | Lsun | float64 |
evals.expected_output: Band magnitude columns map to Photometry.magnitude, errors to Photometry.magnitude_error, with multi-row ingestion note. Teff and logL map to ModeledParameters.

assertions.text: Column 'name' is mapped to Sources table and source field
Column 'Jmag' is mapped to Photometry table and magnitude field
Column 'eJmag' is mapped to Photometry table and magnitude_error field
Column 'W1mag' is mapped to Photometry table and magnitude field
Column 'Teff' is mapped to ModeledParameters table
Column 'logL' is mapped to ModeledParameters table
The ingestion notes mention that each photometry band becomes a separate row in the Photometry table (one row per band per source, not one column per band)

evals.prompt:
I have a catalog of nearby brown dwarfs with columns from astrodb-parse-data-table below. I want to ingest this into my AstroDB database. What table/field does each column map to?

| Column | Description | Units | Type |
|--------|-------------|-------|------|
| designation | Source name | — | str |
| RA_ICRS | Right ascension ICRS | deg | float64 |
| DE_ICRS | Declination ICRS | deg | float64 |
| dist_pc | Distance | pc | float64 |
| e_dist | Distance uncertainty | pc | float64 |
| vsini | Projected rotational velocity | km/s | float64 |
| e_vsini | vsini uncertainty | km/s | float64 |
| Prot | Rotation period | d | float64 |
| e_Prot | Period uncertainty | d | float64 |
| sep_arcsec | Projected separation from companion | arcsec | float64 |
| companion_name | Name of companion object | — | str |
| Gmag | Gaia G magnitude | mag | float64 |
| BPmag | Gaia BP magnitude | mag | float64 |
| RPmag | Gaia RP magnitude | mag | float64 |
| notes | Free-form notes | — | str |
evals.expected_output: Handles tricky unit conversions (dist→parallax, days→hours), companion columns, Gaia photometry, and notes field.

assertions.text: Column 'designation' is mapped to Sources table and source field
Column 'dist_pc' is flagged as needing conversion — either note about converting to parallax_mas (1000/dist_pc) for Parallaxes table, or suggesting ModeledParameters as alternative
Column 'Prot' is mapped to RotationalParameters table and period_hr field
There is a note about unit conversion for Prot from days to hours
Column 'vsini' is mapped to RotationalParameters table and v_sin_i_kms field
Column 'sep_arcsec' is mapped to CompanionRelationships table and projected_separation_arcsec field
Column 'Gmag' is mapped to Photometry table and magnitude field
Column 'notes' is mapped to a comments field in some table

evals.prompt:
I ran astrodb-parse-data-table on NearbyGalaxies_Jan2021_PUBLIC.fits and want to load it into AstroDB. Here's the column summary — can you map each column to the right AstroDB table and field?

| Column | Description | Units | Type |
|--------|-------------|-------|------|
| GalaxyName | Galaxy name | — | str (16-char) |
| RA | Right Ascension (hours minutes seconds) | — | str (10-char) |
| Dec | Declination (degrees arcminutes arcseconds) | — | str (11-char) |
| EB-V | Reddening E(B-V) | mag | float32 |
| dmod | Distance modulus (m-M)0 | mag | float32 |
| dmod+ | Upper uncertainty on dmod | mag | float32 |
| dmod- | Lower uncertainty on dmod | mag | float32 |
| vh | Heliocentric radial velocity | km / s | float32 |
| vh+ | Upper uncertainty on vh | km / s | float32 |
| vh- | Lower uncertainty on vh | km / s | float32 |
| Vmag | Apparent V magnitude (Vega mags) | mag | float32 |
| Vmag+ | Upper uncertainty on Vmag | mag | float32 |
| Vmag- | Lower uncertainty on Vmag | mag | float32 |
| PA | Position angle | deg | float32 |
| PA+ | Upper uncertainty on PA | deg | float32 |
| PA- | Lower uncertainty on PA | deg | float32 |
| e=1-b/a | Projected ellipticity, e=1-b/a | dimensionless_unscaled | float32 |
| e+ | Upper uncertainty on e=1-b/a | dimensionless_unscaled | float32 |
| e- | Lower uncertainty on e=1-b/a | dimensionless_unscaled | float32 |
| muVo | Central V surface brightness | mag / arcsec2 | float32 |
| muVo+ | Upper uncertainty on muVo | mag / arcsec2 | float32 |
| muVo- | Lower uncertainty on muVo | mag / arcsec2 | float32 |
| rh | Half-light radius measured on major axis | arcmin | float32 |
| rh+ | Upper uncertainty on rh | arcmin | float32 |
| rh- | Lower uncertainty on rh | arcmin | float32 |
| sigma_s | Stellar radial velocity dispersion | km / s | float32 |
| sigma_s+ | Upper uncertainty on sigma_s | km / s | float32 |
| sigma_s- | Lower uncertainty on sigma_s | km / s | float32 |
| vrot_s | Stellar peak/max rotation velocity | km / s | float32 |
| vrot_s+ | Upper uncertainty on vrot_s | km / s | float32 |
| vrot_s- | Lower uncertainty on vrot_s | km / s | float32 |
| MHI | HI mass | solMass | float32 |
| sigma_g | HI radial velocity dispersion | km / s | float32 |
| sigma_g+ | Upper uncertainty on sigma_g | km / s | float32 |
| sigma_g- | Lower uncertainty on sigma_g | km / s | float32 |
| vrot_g | HI peak/max rotation velocity | km / s | float32 |
| vrot_g+ | Upper uncertainty on vrot_g | km / s | float32 |
| vrot_g- | Lower uncertainty on vrot_g | km / s | float32 |
| [Fe/H] | Stellar mean metallicity | dex | float32 |
| feh+ | Upper uncertainty on [Fe/H] | dex | float32 |
| feh- | Lower uncertainty on [Fe/H] | dex | float32 |
| F | Flag | — | int16 |
| pmra | Proper motion in right ascension | mas / yr | float32 |
| epmra+ | Upper uncertainty on pmra | mas / yr | float32 |
| epmra- | Lower uncertainty on pmra | mas / yr | float32 |
| pmdec | Proper motion in declination | mas / yr | float32 |
| epmdec+ | Upper uncertainty on pmdec | mas / yr | float32 |
| epmdec- | Lower uncertainty on pmdec | mas / yr | float32 |
| References | Literature references | — | str (53-char) |
evals.expected_output: Galaxy catalog mapped with astropy-format units. vh (km / s) → RadialVelocities.rv_kms, pmra/pmdec (mas / yr) → ProperMotions, e=1-b/a (dimensionless_unscaled) → Morphology.ellipticity, rh (arcmin) → Morphology.half_light_radius_arcmin, MHI (solMass) → ModeledParameters. F flag column marked Unmatched with user prompt. RA/Dec sexagesimal conversion noted.

assertions.text: Column 'GalaxyName' is mapped to Sources table and source field
Column 'vh' with units 'km / s' (astropy format) is correctly mapped to RadialVelocities table and rv_kms field
Column 'Vmag' is mapped to Photometry table and magnitude field
Column 'pmra' with units 'mas / yr' (astropy format) is mapped to ProperMotions table and pm_ra field
Column 'pmdec' with units 'mas / yr' (astropy format) is mapped to ProperMotions table and pm_dec field
Column '[Fe/H]' is mapped to ModeledParameters table
Column 'e=1-b/a' with units 'dimensionless_unscaled' is mapped to Morphology table and ellipticity field
Column 'rh' is mapped to Morphology table and half_light_radius_arcmin field
Column 'F' (flag, int16) is marked Unmatched and the output asks the user what to do with it (options like pre-ingestion filtering or Sources.comments)
The output notes that RA and Dec are strings in sexagesimal format and need conversion to decimal degrees before ingestion
The output notes that asymmetric error columns (e.g. dmod+/dmod-) are non-standard for AstroDB and explains handling options

evals.prompt:
I ran astrodb-parse-data-table on arecibo_radar_observed_objects_list.csv and got this column summary. Can you map each column to the right AstroDB table and field?

| Column | Description | Units | Type |
|--------|-------------|-------|------|
| Number | Identifier number of the observed object | — | str |
| Name | Common name of the observed object | — | str |
| Designation | Provisional or permanent designation (e.g., asteroid number) | — | str |
| Category | Category/type of object (e.g., Planet, Moon, MBA, Comet) | — | str |
| Components | Number of components in the system | dimensionless_unscaled | float64 |
| Absolute Magnitute | Absolute magnitude of the object (note: column name contains typo — should be "Magnitude") | mag | float64 |
| Quality Code for Best Images | Quality code for best radar images (numeric scale) | — | float64 |
| NASA Link | URL link to the NASA page for the object | — | str |
evals.expected_output: Solar system catalog: identifiers map to Sources/Names, Absolute Magnitute marked Unmatched (absolute not apparent mag), NASA Link marked Unmatched (generic URL), Quality Code marked Unmatched (quality flag). User is asked what to do with all three Unmatched columns.

assertions.text: At least one of Name/Designation/Number is mapped to Sources.source as a primary identifier

Column 'Absolute Magnitute' is NOT mapped directly to Photometry.magnitude without a caveat — it is either marked Unmatched or mapped to ModeledParameters, with a note explaining that AstroDB Photometry stores apparent magnitudes only
Column 'NASA Link' is marked Unmatched and the output asks the user what to do with the URL (options like Sources.comments, Spectra.access_url, or skip)
Column 'Quality Code for Best Images' is marked Unmatched and the output asks the user what to do (options like pre-ingestion filtering or skip)
At least 3 of the 8 columns are marked Unmatched or flagged with strong caveats — this is a solar system catalog and most columns fall outside AstroDB's stellar schema
